title: Write a self-rescheduling worker (reschedule FIRST, then run)
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.HeartbeatWorker` (queue `:monitoring`, max_attempts 3) that sends a heartbeat every 10 minutes by self-rescheduling. Per Sorentwo's 'Reliable Scheduling' recipe, the reschedule must happen at the start of perform/1 (before the business logic), otherwise retries will cause exponential growth of scheduled jobs. Use a pattern-match clause on `%Oban.Job{attempt: 1}` to reschedule only on the first attempt. perform/1 takes `%{"host" => host}` and calls `MyApp.Monitor.ping(host)`. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/heartbeat_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: The reschedule-first ordering is specific to the Sorentwo recipe and non-obvious. Reschedule-at-end is the default-LLM answer and is wrong.
research_citation: Sorentwo 'Oban Recipes Part 3: Reliable Scheduling' — 'we'll keep retrying the job's business logic when the job retries, but we'll only schedule the next occurrence once.'
